field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: The critical condition of liquid chromatography is the transition point of the separation mode between size exclusion and adsorption (or partition) when a porous column packing material is used for the separation of macromolecules. At the critical point of a particular polymer species, retention of that polymer becomes independent of the molecular weight.
field_evidence.critical_component: the critical condition of PEO block
field_evidence.column_name: a reversed-phase column (LUNA C18, 100-Å pore size, 250 × 4.6 mm)
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: a mixture of acetonitrile (ACN, Aldrich HPLC grade) and water (deionized and filtered with a 0.45- _µ_ m filter)
field_evidence.mobile_phase_ratio: an ACN/water mixture at the ratio of 60/40 (v/ v)

conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, Pohang University of Science and Technology, Pohang, 790-784, Korea
conditions.notes: Condition established for diblock copolymer analysis.
conditions.detector: refractive index detector; UV-visible absorption detector
